copper.note: Copper input materials (ores, concentrates, cathodes, anodes) and scrap are currently outside Section 232 — these rates apply to semi-finished copper and copper-intensive derivatives.

section301.note: Section 301 stacks on top of Section 232 for Chinese-origin goods. 25% is typical for steel/aluminum products; your exact rate depends on 301 list placement.

tradedeal.note: Trade-deal partners (EU, UK, Japan, Korea, Taiwan, etc.) are generally capped at a 15% total duty outcome.

uk.note: UK-origin metal qualifies for reduced rates under the Economic Prosperity Deal, pending ongoing talks.

usmca.note: For USMCA-qualifying goods, duty applies only to non-U.S. content. Minimum-rate rules may raise the effective outcome — confirm with your broker.

australia.note: Australia is currently exempt from Section 232 steel/aluminum duties.

russia.note: Aluminum of Russian origin (or smelted/cast in Russia) carries a 200% ad valorem duty.
